record.field: KitchenOwl is a self-hosted grocery list and recipe manager that keeps your household's shopping, cooking and meal planning in sync across every device.

KitchenOwl is a self-hosted grocery list and recipe manager that keeps your household's shopping, cooking and meal planning in sync across every device.

It combines real-time shopping lists, a recipe library, weekly meal planning and shared expense tracking into a single household-oriented app. Built with a Flask backend and a Flutter frontend, it ships native clients for mobile, web and desktop, with partial offline support so the list keeps working without signal. It is designed for families or shared homes that want to coordinate groceries and costs while keeping all data on their own server.

Key features
  • Real-time shopping lists with categories, quantities and icons, synced across multiple users
  • Recipe management with import from the web, custom recipes and one-tap add of ingredients to the list
  • Weekly meal planning tied to your recipe collection
  • Shared household expense tracking with balances and cost splitting between members
  • Native apps for Android, iOS, web, macOS, Windows and Linux, with partial offline support
  • Smart suggestions that learn shopping habits and recipe preferences over time
Best for

Families and shared households that want to coordinate groceries, recipes, meal plans and expenses in real time while self-hosting their data and avoiding cloud grocery apps.

services: web: image: tombursch/kitchenowl:v0.7.1 restart: unless-stopped environment: - JWT_SECRET_KEY={{ jwt_secret_key }} - DB_DRIVER=postgresql - DB_HOST=db - DB_NAME={{ db_name }} - DB_USER={{ db_user }} - DB_PASSWORD={{ db_password }} volumes: - ./kitchenowl_files:/data depends_on: - db container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: postgres:17 restart: unless-stopped environment: - POSTGRES_DB={{ db_name }} - POSTGRES_USER={{ db_user }} - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ db_password }} volumes: - ./kitchenowl_db:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -d $${POSTGRES_DB} -U $${POSTGRES_USER} interval: 30s timeout: 60s retries: 5 start_period: 80s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
